thesis_fit: AI agent + operator: the hardening shipped canonical versioning but exposed no policed path to read history on kernel/SDK/MCP/CLI; reading prior versions forces /unsafe (bypassing gate + redaction). Removes hand-walking provenance and the /unsafe escape on all surfaces.
scope: src/contracts/canonical-store.ts (signatures present); src/kernel/cluster-kernel-interface.ts + cluster-kernel.ts + policy-enforced-kernel.ts (gated listEntityVersions/getEntityVersion); src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts; src/mcp/server.ts (cluster_list_versions/cluster_get_version + annotations); src/mcp/sanitize.ts (historical-entity sanitizer); src/cli.ts (db-cluster versions <id> [--at <n>] [--json]); docs/mcp.md
dependencies: HARD: each historical revision is owner-truth and MUST pass the same read_owner_truth capability check + identical redaction/sanitizeEntityForOutput as live resolve; never raw rows; reading a prior version requires the same capability as the current version. Implement at kernel once; one SDK accessor unblocks CLI+MCP. (merged from FEAT-AI-001, FEAT-SDK-001, FEAT-CLI-006)
recommendation: Build for v2.0. MCP tools readOnly:true with empty_reason; SDK typed records + JSDoc; CLI wrapper small once accessor lands.

thesis_fit: AI agent: the thesis core. search() is whole-query substring containment with zero ranking; NL queries return nothing or arbitrary order. Biggest promise/behavior gap.
scope: src/adapters/local/local-index-store.ts (query-time tokenization + scoring); src/indexing/tokenizer.ts (reuse tokenize + TF/doc-length table); src/contracts/index-store.ts + src/types/index-record.ts (score); src/types/evidence-bundle.ts + src/retrieval/retrieval-planner.ts (thread score)
dependencies: Reuse existing tokenizer at query time. TF/doc-length implies index-format bump + rebuild; rebuildIndex already does atomic replaceAll (no owner-truth migration). Pairs with FEAT-RETR-004.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 as the headline retrieval feature. BM25 (k1~1.2,b~0.75), pure-TS, no deps, local. Makes full-text TRUE (resolves half of FEAT-RETR-003). Defer vector to v2.x.

thesis_fit: Operator: real diagnostics. verify/doctor/health report corruption but exit 0, silently passing CI/cron. Makes `verify && deploy` safe.
scope: src/cli.ts handleVerify (316-337), handleDoctor (341-362), handleHealth (434-461)
dependencies: none — result objects carry ok/status; CLI fails to act. Pairs with FEAT-CLI-008.
recommendation: v2.0. if(!result.ok) process.exitCode=1 per handler. Single highest-leverage operator fix; ship regardless. Retract operations.md:58 'OR ok:false' workaround after.

thesis_fit: Operator: only canonical is durable on Postgres; artifact/index/ledger silently fall back to single-node files even when backend=postgres, so the provenance ledger is the least scalable store. Removes 'I chose Postgres but my event log still corrupts under parallel writers.'
scope: src/adapters/postgres/ (postgres-artifact-store.ts, postgres-index-store.ts, postgres-ledger-store.ts); schema.ts + migrations 003/004/005; src/adapters/factory.ts; src/adapters/index.ts. Artifact content: bytea vs LO vs FS-pointer
dependencies: Needs FEAT-STORE-007 migration runner to be usable without hand-applied DDL. Ledger store must use a real serial sequence for monotonic seq. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE storage headline with FEAT-STORE-006 — pick one.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 IF Postgres is the chosen storage headline. Sequence ledger->index->artifact. bytea for small/medium blobs + documented ceiling; leave door open to S3 pointer (FEAT-STORE-010). Honestly disclosed today (not a claim-mismatch).

thesis_fit: AI agent: next_valid_actions is advertised as walking the agent to the next step but is computed only for the 7 lifecycle tools and never by redactError, so POLICY_DENIED/CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH/non-lifecycle errors lack it. Central agent-autonomy friction.
scope: src/mcp/sanitize.ts (redactError code->actions table); src/mcp/server.ts:1372-1391 (apply for all tools); docs/mcp.md (presence guarantee)
dependencies: none — additive; lifecycleNextValidActions map exists; add code-keyed defaults (POLICY_DENIED->cluster_policy_explain, CONTENT_HASH_MISMATCH->cluster_propose_mutation, NOT_FOUND->cluster_find_sources).
recommendation: Build for v2.0. Compute for every tool; document present-when-deterministic-next-tool-exists. Highest-leverage small fix in the domain.

thesis_fit: AI agent: two on-wire error shapes coexist; the two INJECT-001 gate refusals (commit/compensate — core of safe mutation lifecycle) return a bare body with no isError/content[], so the documented JSON.parse(content[0].text) recipe breaks on exactly those.
scope: src/mcp/server.ts:982-1018 (commit gate), 1057-1082 (compensate gate); docs/mcp.md:199-280
dependencies: Pick ONE canonical wire shape. Gates currently return from inside handleTool, not the request handler.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 (breaking permitted). Gates throw typed PolicyDeniedError so the single catch-arm path yields the one documented shape. Pairs with FEAT-AI-003.

thesis_fit: AI agent/MCP host: ListTools emits a private 5-field annotation vocab while docs promise spec readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint, so generic hosts can't auto-gate commit/compensate as destructive.
scope: src/mcp/server.ts:352-365 (ToolAnnotations+AnnotatedTool), 1241-1249 (ListTools builder); docs/mcp.md:136-153
dependencies: Keep internal 5-field; also emit spec hints derived from it (readOnlyHint=readOnly; destructiveHint=writesCluster true only for commit/compensate; idempotentHint=true for pure reads+reject).
recommendation: Build for v2.0. Emit both — spec hints under annotations on all 16 tools, internal flags under a namespaced key. Makes annotation-completeness answerable yes.

thesis_fit: AI agent: vector retrieval is ABSENT not partial; embedding?:number[] is never written/read and no vector math exists. Conceptual recall (access control vs permissions/RBAC) returns nothing.
scope: src/types/index-record.ts; pluggable LOCAL embedder seam (operator-supplied, no egress); src/adapters/local/local-index-store.ts (vector scoring + hybrid merge with BM25); src/indexing/content-indexer.ts + src/ops/rebuild.ts (embeddings at index time); src/retrieval/retrieval-planner.ts (hybrid)
dependencies: Land AFTER FEAT-RETR-001 so vector is the second signal. Brute-force cosine fine below ~10k records. Must ship as pluggable local embedder (no-egress). Index-format bump + rebuild. Gates FEAT-AI-008.
recommendation: Defer to v2.x. For 2.0 pair FEAT-RETR-001 + FEAT-RETR-003 retraction; deliver hybrid lexical+vector as flagship v2.1/2.2. Sequence: RETR-001->RETR-002->AI-008.

thesis_fit: Trust is the product: 'full-text/vector lookup' is promised but neither is delivered; the repo self-contradicts (retrieval-bundles.md:3 is honest).
claim_location: README.md:62; src/contracts/index-store.ts:5; docs/store-contracts.md:52 (+ 8 translated READMEs at line 62)
scope: README.md:62, src/contracts/index-store.ts:5, docs/store-contracts.md:52 (one commit), then regenerate 8 README translations
dependencies: Implement-or-retract twin of FEAT-RETR-001/002.
recommendation: Decide in lockstep with RETR-001/002. Ship BM25 -> claim becomes 'full-text lookup (ranked), metadata search'; drop 'vector' (roadmap). Do NOT ship 2.0 with current wording. Regen translations before tag/publish.

thesis_fit: AI agent: the EvidenceBundle gives no relevance score and no citable snippet, so grounded attributable answers force re-fetch + re-find. ResolvedEvidence has neither.
scope: src/types/evidence-bundle.ts (score + snippet); src/retrieval/retrieval-planner.ts (populate score, extract window via integrity-checked getContent); src/cli.ts (~1697-1720); src/mcp/server.ts (cluster_retrieve_bundle returns scores); docs/retrieval-bundles.md (doc-drift typechecks code blocks — same PR)
dependencies: Depends on FEAT-RETR-001 (without a ranker score is degenerate). Snippet reads artifact content at plan time.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 alongside FEAT-RETR-001. Add score (always) + snippet (artifacts). Keep additive.

thesis_fit: App dev + AI agent: no cursor/limit on list/search; unbounded results are a memory/latency cliff past toy size.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (signatures); src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts (thread limit/cursor through the gate); index/canonical store list/search contracts. Return {items,nextCursor}
dependencies: Co-design the query-options object with FEAT-SDK-006. Gate+redaction per page. Opaque cursor (no internal IDs).
recommendation: Build for v2.0 (MAJOR — break acceptable; note in changelog). Stable opaque cursor + default page size.

thesis_fit: AI agent + operator: provenance/receipt spine is the differentiator and the lifecycle ends in read-receipt, but missing typed getReceipt/walkLineage pushes the auditing agent into /unsafe.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (typed getReceipt/getLineage); src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts (policed ledger reads if absent)
dependencies: VERIFY FIRST whether a policed ledger read path already exists (D3 had suppressed tool output). Read-side only — NOT the externally-anchored/signed ledger (out of scope).
recommendation: Build for v2.0 if not present (drop if it exists). Typed Receipt/Lineage with integrity as a typed field + JSDoc. With FEAT-VERSIONS-001 this discharges most of FEAT-SDK-008.

thesis_fit: Operator/app-dev: documented --version/-v falls through to help; version is the first thing in any bug report/pin.
scope: src/cli.ts parseArgs/main switch; read version from package.json
dependencies: none
recommendation: v2.0. Print package.json version (plain + {version} under --json). Implement, do not retract.

thesis_fit: Operator: multi-minute rebuild/verify/backup print nothing — indistinguishable from a hang on a 100k-entry store. Seeded V2-C1-009.
scope: src/ops/{rebuild,verify,backup,restore,migrations}.ts + cli.ts handlers; root cause sdk/index.ts:58-60 (whole-array, no progress events)
dependencies: Two-tier. CHEAP: rebuild already loops per-entry -> N/M stderr counter under --progress now. FULL: verify/backup/exportAll need streaming SDK variants (SDK signature change; pairs with FEAT-CLI-009). Share one progress event shape with FEAT-AI-004.
recommendation: v2.0 ship the cheap tier (rebuild+migrate stderr counters, TTY-aware). Defer streaming verify/backup to v2.x behind SDK streaming.

thesis_fit: Operator: the kernel already emits structured OperatorSignals (integrity/policy/degradation/receipt/conflict) into a working ring buffer, but no surface drains them, so every safety event is invisible. Makes the v1.x security spine observable. Seeded KERNEL-C-012.
scope: src/kernel/operator-signal.ts (drain/peek implemented); SDK accessor drainSignals/peekSignals (absent, sdk/index.ts:69-70); new db-cluster signals [--drain] [--json] in cli.ts
dependencies: SDK accessor first (small — buffer + drain/peek already exist). Then thin CLI command. No engine work.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 — pull it in. Hard part already shipped; only the surface is missing. signals (peek default, --drain to clear, --json, --since/--severity polish). Retract operations.md 'planned' note. Do NOT defer.

thesis_fit: Operator/app-dev: README describes a runnable viewer-only React dashboard demo in dashboard/, but no .tsx/.jsx exists anywhere; only view-models exist in src/dashboard/.
scope: dashboard/ (only apply-redaction.d.ts today); src/dashboard/*.ts (view-models built); README.md:77-83
dependencies: A real UI needs the view-models surfaced on the SDK (FEAT-STORE-009) or must import internals.
recommendation: Implement-or-retract — recommend RETRACT for v2.0 (reword to 'view-models live in src/dashboard/; no reference UI bundled', delete the orphan .d.ts). A thin static viewer is v2.x polish. Do NOT ship 2.0 with the false text.

thesis_fit: Operator: TLS was honestly retracted in the hardening pass (not a current claim-mismatch); today the connection string is passed to pg as-is and a forgotten sslmode silently ships plaintext credentials. Safe writes over an unencrypted link is a hole.
scope: src/contracts/config.ts (typed ssl: boolean|{ca,rejectUnauthorized,...}); src/adapters/factory.ts:49 (pass ssl into Pool); src/util/config-loader.ts (env+file, DB_CLUSTER_PG_SSL); SECURITY.md
dependencies: Land with FEAT-STORE-008 (pool config) — same Pool construction + config schema; one connection-hardening pass.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 with explicitness + observability: accept a structured validated ssl option and have db-cluster doctor report the resolved TLS mode of the live connection. The prior impl failed by being implicit.

thesis_fit: AI agent + operator: the ledger is the provenance spine and most write-heavy store; LocalLedgerStore rewrites the WHOLE events.json on every append (O(n) per append, O(n^2) to build) with a non-atomic write admitted not atomic under parallel writers. A long agent risks a torn ledger.
scope: src/adapters/local/local-ledger-store.ts (append path/format); same pattern in local-index-store.ts:34. NDJSON append (O(1)) or SQLite-backed ledger (FEAT-STORE-006)
dependencies: If SQLite, folds into FEAT-STORE-006. seq must stay monotonic without reading the whole file. Provide a one-time events.json->NDJSON converter.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 if a real workload is expected — worst offender, most thesis-critical. Cheapest: NDJSON append (no new dep); cleaner: SQLite if FEAT-STORE-006 greenlit.

thesis_fit: AI agent: once history is readable, 'what changed' is the next question; today the agent fetches both objects and diffs in-context (token-expensive, error-prone on nested attrs). Server-side diff makes a committed mutation's result legible.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (diff helper over canonical objects); src/mcp/server.ts (1 read-only tool); reuses FEAT-VERSIONS-001 gated read + entity sanitizer
dependencies: Depends on FEAT-VERSIONS-001. Diff of two SANITIZED values (never raw) so a redacted field can't leak via a delta.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 IF FEAT-VERSIONS-001 lands; else defer. cluster_diff {uri,fromVersion,toVersion} (optionally {uriA,uriB}) -> {added,removed,changed:[{path,before,after}]} sanitized. Canonical-only; artifact-content diff out.

thesis_fit: Operator via agent: doctor/verify/rebuild/backup/restore run for minutes as single-shot blocking calls; an agent can't budget a timeout or tell working from hung.
scope: src/ops/{doctor,verify,rebuild,backup,restore}.ts (onProgress callback); src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (expose progress + ops verbs); src/mcp/server.ts (job-handle if exposed); docs/mcp.md
dependencies: MCP stdio has no server->model push, so the realistic 2.0 shape is job-handle+poll (cluster_op_start->{jobId}; cluster_op_status->{phase,pct,done,result?}), needs an in-process job registry. Shares one progress event shape with FEAT-CLI-004. The 5 ops are NOT on the current 16-tool surface.
recommendation: Phase it. v2.0: onProgress through the ops layer + SDK/CLI cheap tier. Defer MCP job-handle/poll to v2.1 unless the maintenance ops are added to MCP in 2.0. Do not fake streaming over stdio.

thesis_fit: AI agent: ingesting N artifacts is up to 4N MCP round-trips each burning a model turn — dominant cost + abandonment risk. Batch serves safe mutation lifecycle at agent-realistic volume without weakening per-command gating.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (batch wrappers); src/mcp/server.ts (cluster_propose_mutations [plural], cluster_resolve_many); docs/mcp.md
dependencies: Each item keeps its own lifecycle + per-item policy gate + per-item validation (transport optimization, not approval bypass). Per-item {ok|error}. Keep commit per-command so INJECT-001 gate is never diluted. Related to FEAT-SDK-003 (SDK batch incl. commit — the superset).
recommendation: Build for v2.0: cluster_propose_mutations {commands:[...]}->[{commandId|error}] and cluster_resolve_many {uris:[...]} per-URI sanitized + per-item empty_reason. Hold batch-commit for v2.x.

thesis_fit: AI agent: agents phrase needs conceptually; empty no_match on semantically-present-but-lexically-absent content forces many reformulations. The agent-facing tool to expose vector retrieval lives here.
scope: src/mcp/server.ts (cluster_search_semantic, read-only, sanitized like find_sources); src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts
dependencies: GATED ON FEAT-RETR-002 — no vector capability exists today. Thin wrapper but cannot ship before the vector index. MUST reuse the same policy gate + sanitizers + empty_reason as find_sources (all_filtered_by_policy must distinguish no-match from policy-stripped).
recommendation: Defer to v2.x, contingent on FEAT-RETR-002. When it lands: cluster_search_semantic {query,limit?,minScore?} -> find_sources shape + per-hit score, a SIBLING of find_sources. Sequence: RETR-001->RETR-002->AI-008.

thesis_fit: AI agent: cluster_inspect_command reads ONE command by ID but there is no tool to LIST commands by status; an agent that proposed several or resumed after a context reset is blind to its own pending work queue. list_receipts covers committed history only.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (list pending by status); src/mcp/server.ts (cluster_list_commands {status?,proposedBy?,limit?}, read-only); src/kernel/command-queue.ts (read path); docs/mcp.md
dependencies: Reuse formatCommandOutput (as inspect does) so listing inherits the same field exposure; gate like inspect. Command queue already persists pending commands. No schema migration.
recommendation: Build for v2.0. cluster_list_commands {status?,proposedBy?,limit?} -> array of formatCommandOutput + empty_reason. Pairs with FEAT-VERSIONS-001 (list approved, resume committing). Closes the read-side lifecycle gap.

thesis_fit: App dev + AI agent: IndexQuery is {text?,sourceStore?,metadata?,limit?} — no field-scoped query (filename/heading vs body), no phrase vs loose terms, no pagination beyond limit. Forces client-side over-fetch + filter.
scope: src/contracts/index-store.ts (fields?, phrase?, offset?); src/adapters/local/local-index-store.ts (honor them); src/types/index-record.ts (zoned text); src/retrieval/retrieval-planner.ts + src/cli.ts + src/mcp/server.ts
dependencies: Field-scoped is most valuable with zoned text stored separately (index-format change + rebuild). Builds on FEAT-RETR-001's ranker (phrase/field as scoring modifiers).
recommendation: Land offset (pagination) in v2.0 — tiny, removes an agent-iteration footgun. Defer fields/phrase to v2.x; bundle the zoned-text format change with the FEAT-RETR-002 rebuild. Don't gold-plate boolean operators.

thesis_fit: AI agent: sourceStore includes 'ledger' as valid but the planner resolves only canonical+artifact, so ledger index hits fall through both branches — neither resolved nor reported as missingContext, silently vanishing. Dead path for the provenance thesis.
scope: src/retrieval/retrieval-planner.ts (ledger branch OR MissingContext for any unhandled sourceStore); src/contracts/index-store.ts (clarify ledger support); src/ops/rebuild.ts + src/indexing (nothing indexes ledger content today)
dependencies: Decision-first: is ledger content-discoverable? Yes -> indexing path + resolve branch (medium). No -> drop 'ledger' from union OR emit MissingContext. Silent-drop closure is independently shippable + small.
recommendation: v2.0 the small safety fix regardless — emit MissingContext (impact:medium) for any unresolved sourceStore so the bundle never silently omits a hit. Defer full ledger-content retrieval to v2.x; if undecided, remove 'ledger' from the union.

thesis_fit: Operator + app dev: LocalIndexStore loads the whole index into a Map, rewrites the WHOLE file on every index()/remove(), and full-scans per query; per-record indexing is O(N^2) bytes. factory.ts:116 always uses the JSON-file index even under Postgres.
scope: src/adapters/local/local-index-store.ts (batch/debounced persistence; off full-file rewrite); src/adapters/factory.ts:116 (Postgres/SQLite-FTS index adapter); src/contracts/index-store.ts (replaceAll exists; gap is the single-record write path)
dependencies: A SQLite-FTS5 index adapter solves scale + ranking (RETR-001) + field/phrase (RETR-005) in one move (FEAT-STORE-006). Interim: debounced/batched persist.
recommendation: Defer the big adapter to v2.x — do NOT block v2.0. For 2.0 ensure bulk indexing goes through replaceAll (it does in rebuild) and document the single-file ceiling. SQLite-FTS5 is the highest-leverage retrieval bet; weigh BM25-in-JS (now, disposable) vs SQLite-FTS (durable v2.x centerpiece).

thesis_fit: AI agent: bulk ingest/commit pays per-call gate + receipt + round-trip N times with no grouping — the throughput bottleneck of the safe mutation lifecycle.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (ingestMany/commitMany); src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts (batched gate eval preserving one receipt per item + per-item provenance)
dependencies: Hard: partial-failure semantics; gate per item; each item its own integrity-checked receipt; per-item success/failure, never all-or-nothing throw. Build after FEAT-VERSIONS-001/FEAT-SDK-002. Superset of FEAT-AI-005 (MCP batch).
recommendation: Defer to v2.x unless agent bulk-ingest is a near-term named workload. Return typed BatchResult[] with per-item receipt or typed error. (FEAT-AI-005 captures propose/read savings in 2.0 without commit-batch risk.)

thesis_fit: AI agent + app dev: only get-by-id + free-text search forces over-fetch-then-filter client-side or a drop into /unsafe for 'entities where field=X/status=Y'. Gated filtering keeps efficient retrieval inside the safe surface.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (query options); src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts (push predicates down so filtering never sees redacted-away fields); index/canonical store contracts
dependencies: Share the query-options object with FEAT-SDK-002 — design together. Predicates on redacted fields must be rejected/empty, never evaluated against raw values.
recommendation: Defer to v2.x, co-design with pagination. Keep the predicate surface small (equality + a few operators on whitelisted fields); reject a full query language as bloat.

thesis_fit: App dev: loose/unknown return shapes force casts and lose compile-time safety. A generic ClusterSDK<TSchema> gives app code well-typed records from ingest/retrieve/version reads.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts type signatures; src/index.ts exported types
dependencies: VERIFY current type strength first (D3 had suppressed tool output). If already strongly typed, downgrade to a JSDoc/examples polish item.
recommendation: Verify, then build in v2.0 if shapes are loose — add generics alongside the version-history/receipt types. If already typed, drop.

thesis_fit: App dev: if standing up a policed cluster requires hand-assembling principal + policy + capabilities + backend with thin defaults, first-integration friction pushes people toward /unsafe to just get something working.
scope: src/index.ts (createSafeCluster signature + defaults); config types
dependencies: VERIFY current factory shape (suppressed tool output). Additive (defaults + overloads).
recommendation: Verify, then add a defaulted overload in v2.0 if wiring is heavy (createSafeCluster({backend:'local'}) with a sensible default principal/policy + typed Postgres opts). Never default to an over-permissive policy. If already clean, collapses to a quickstart doc item.

thesis_fit: App dev + AI agent: every legitimate flow forced into /unsafe (version history today; likely lineage/receipt reads) is a hole in the safe surface AND, if package-boundary.md asserts sufficiency, a doc promise the code doesn't keep.
claim_location: docs/package-boundary.md — line UNVERIFIED (D3 tool output suppressed; owner must locate the exact 'safe surface covers X' assertion)
scope: docs/package-boundary.md, docs/sdk.md, export surface src/index.ts vs src/unsafe.ts
dependencies: Largely resolved by shipping FEAT-VERSIONS-001 + FEAT-SDK-009; re-audit the boundary doc after.
recommendation: Implement-or-retract — sequence after VERSIONS-001 + SDK-009, then update the doc to reflect the complete safe surface OR explicitly document remaining intentional /unsafe-only ops. Do NOT ship 2.0 with a 'safe surface complete' claim while version history is /unsafe-only.

thesis_fit: Operator/app-dev: docs promise color auto-disables on non-TTY but code only disables on explicit flags, so piping get id > file embeds raw ANSI and corrupts logs/parsers.
scope: src/cli/color-output.ts (no isTTY/NO_COLOR/FORCE_COLOR) + cli.ts:79 createColorOutput call
dependencies: none. Shares isTTY plumbing with FEAT-CLI-011.
recommendation: v2.0. Gate color on process.stdout.isTTY && !NO_COLOR, honor FORCE_COLOR. Implement, do not retract. (Separate latent help-output bug noted for the bug-pass, not here.)

thesis_fit: Operator: health answers 'up?' but never 'how well?' — no read/write/denial counters, no latency, no entity/mutation/receipt counts. In-process, pull-based, zero egress.
scope: src/ops/health.ts (point-in-time only) + a kernel counters source. Surface as db-cluster health --metrics --json and/or db-cluster stats. Denials already flow through the kernel + emit OperatorSignals
dependencies: Lightweight in-kernel counters (reads,writes,mutations,denials,errors). No schema migration. Synergizes with FEAT-CLI-005 (aggregates the denial signals).
recommendation: v2.0 for cluster-wide COUNTS via a stats command — cheap, high operator value, mostly aggregation. Defer latency histograms + OpenMetrics to v2.x. Strictly local; not egress telemetry.

thesis_fit: Operator/app-dev automating the CLI: --json error is {error:'free text'} with no code, so scripts can't branch on error TYPE; exit is binary 0/1 so CI can't react differently to config vs integrity vs store-unavailable. OPS_ERRORS codes exist but are unused.
scope: src/ops/errors.ts (OPS_ERRORS + OpsError.code/hint) + cli.ts fail() (~390-397). Emit {error:{code,message,hint}} under --json + a small exit-code map
dependencies: none in code. Pairs with FEAT-CLI-001.
recommendation: v2.0 (take the breaking shape change now). Adopt OPS_ERRORS codes in fail(), populate hint, define a documented exit-code scheme (2=usage,3=config,4=store-unavailable,5=integrity). Document the catalog in docs/cli.md.

thesis_fit: Operator: rebuild is not resumable — interrupted at 50k of 100k, the next run starts from scratch, wasting a multi-minute run on a transient interruption. Graceful degradation for the primary recovery path.
scope: src/ops/rebuild.ts (no checkpoint); backup.ts/restore.ts (full-only). Needs a checkpoint cursor persisted between runs
dependencies: Depends on SDK streaming/cursor work (sdk/index.ts:58-60 whole-array) — same dependency as FEAT-CLI-004 full tier; build together.
recommendation: Defer to v2.x. Real value but large and gated on the SDK streaming refactor. The FEAT-CLI-004 cheap-tier counter addresses the acute is-it-hung pain; resumability is the follow-on. Bundle with streaming verify/backup.

thesis_fit: App dev + AI agent: store-contracts.md:23 claims 'Large blobs supported' but getContent reads the whole file into a Buffer and put() takes a fully-materialized Buffer/string, so a multi-hundred-MB model output is loaded entirely into Node heap — OOM risk + latency spike.
scope: src/contracts/artifact-store.ts (putStream(Readable,mediaType), getContentStream(id)->Readable); src/adapters/local/local-artifact-store.ts (createReadStream/createWriteStream + streaming sha256 transform); src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts:73-96 (wrapArtifact to gate+receipt streaming paths)
dependencies: Content-hash-as-id means hashing the stream as written; id known only after completion -> write-to-temp-then-rename for atomicity. Pairs with S3 (FEAT-STORE-010). Overlaps FEAT-SDK-007 at the SDK layer.
recommendation: Add streaming as an additive contract method for v2.0 (keep buffer paths for small artifacts). Either implement true streaming or soften 'Large blobs supported' to a practical ceiling. Recommend implementing — model outputs imply large artifacts are primary.

thesis_fit: App dev + operator: a hard gap between local (single-node, non-atomic, O(n)) and Postgres (needs a server, canonical-only). A single-file SQLite adapter gives ACID, real indexes, FTS5, monotonic rowids, WAL concurrency — all four stores at parity in one adapter, no server.
scope: new src/adapters/sqlite/ (four stores + schema); src/contracts/config.ts (backend enum + sqlitePath); src/adapters/factory.ts (wire). FTS5 virtual table satisfies the index store's pluggable-richer-backend promise
dependencies: Adds a native dep (better-sqlite3 or node:sqlite on Node 22+). Overlaps FEAT-STORE-004 (SQLite ledger fixes append for free) and FEAT-STORE-011 (FTS5 = full-text). MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE storage headline with FEAT-STORE-001.
recommendation: Strong candidate but scope-heavy — pick ONE of {Postgres parity (STORE-001), SQLite adapter} as the v2.0 storage headline. Recommend evaluating SQLite FIRST (full four-store parity + atomic ledger + FTS5 in one server-less adapter best serves the embed-a-trustworthy-cluster thesis). If user base is server-deployed, prioritize Postgres. Explicit product decision required.

thesis_fit: Operator: schema.ts:6-10 says there is no migration runner; operators apply SQL by hand, track which ran, redo per environment — and a forgotten 002_canonical_versions silently breaks listVersions/getVersion at runtime. No docker-compose, no .env example, no db-cluster migrate.
scope: new db-cluster migrate subcommand (src/cli/index.ts — help comment at :11-12 already advertises unimplemented subcommands); a schema_migrations table; a runner applying pending files from src/adapters/postgres/migrations/ in order. Optionally docker-compose.yml + .env.example
dependencies: More important once FEAT-STORE-001 adds artifact/index/ledger tables. Build the runner before or with parity.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 alongside Postgres parity (if Postgres is the headline). A ~50-line runner + schema_migrations table removes the friction; ship docker-compose.yml so an operator stands up Postgres + runs migrations in two commands.

thesis_fit: Operator: the Pool is constructed with only {connectionString} — no max/idleTimeoutMillis/connectionTimeoutMillis/statement timeout and no pool.on('error') handler (an unhandled idle-connection error can crash the process); the config schema has nowhere to set them. For production-grade an operator can't tune or harden the connection.
scope: src/contracts/config.ts (optional pool block: max,idleTimeoutMillis,connectionTimeoutMillis,statement_timeout); src/adapters/factory.ts:44-57 (thread into Pool + attach pool.on('error')); optional bounded retry in postgres-canonical-store.ts
dependencies: Shares config-surface + Pool-construction change with FEAT-STORE-003 (TLS) — do both in one pass. Retry must respect the FOR UPDATE/BEGIN tx in put() (retry at the operation boundary).
recommendation: Build for v2.0 with FEAT-STORE-003 as one Postgres connection-hardening change. At minimum expose pool sizing + timeouts and attach the pool error handler (prevents process-crash on idle-connection errors). pg Pool already reconnects lazily; prioritize the error handler + tunables over a custom retry loop.

thesis_fit: App dev: the SDK exposes only health(); the useful view-models (buildDashboardModel/buildOpsModel/buildInspectorData) exist in src/dashboard/ but aren't reachable from the SDK handle (package exports only '.' and './mcp'), forcing app devs to import internals. CLI and MCP can call these; the SDK consumer is left out.
scope: src/sdk/index.ts (cluster.stats()->DashboardModel, cluster.ops()->OpsModel, cluster.inspect(id)->InspectorData delegating to existing builders); docs/SDK.md
dependencies: none — builders exist and take ClusterStores; SDK holds the policy-wrapped stores. CAVEAT: SDK stats()/inspect() should go through the policy-enforced facade; CLI builders currently run on RAW stores (cli/index.ts:20,26,32 call buildStores directly, bypassing the kernel) — align the SDK path to the enforced stores. A real bundled dashboard (FEAT-STORE-002) would consume these.
recommendation: Build for v2.0 — small, high-leverage symmetry fix; view-models already used by CLI/MCP. Route through the enforced stores.

thesis_fit: App dev + operator: large immutable artifacts are retrieved all-or-nothing (memory pressure, no progress); long ops (verify/doctor) return only at completion. Streaming/iterables make large-artifact retrieval + long diagnostics usable in real apps.
scope: src/sdk/cluster-sdk.ts (AsyncIterable/stream for artifact reads + long ops); kernel + artifact store contract
dependencies: Streaming threaded through the artifact store contract + the gate (authorize before the stream opens; define integrity-check semantics on a streamed artifact). Build after pagination establishes iteration conventions. Overlaps FEAT-STORE-005 at the store layer.
recommendation: Defer to v2.x. Weakest item on the SDK list — real pain but a large architectural lift. Tracked follow-up; reassess once a concrete large-artifact workload is named.

thesis_fit: Operator: the index-stale runbook is diagnose -> manually rebuild -> manually re-verify; doctor detects a stale index but takes no action. A guarded --fix that rebuilds a detected-stale index collapses three manual steps into one.
scope: src/ops/doctor.ts + cli.ts handleDoctor. Limit --fix to provably-safe non-destructive remediations (index rebuild); never auto-touch ledger/canonical
dependencies: Builds on rebuild (exists). Require explicit --fix (never default) + ideally --yes. Safer once FEAT-CLI-004 progress exists.
recommendation: Defer to v2.x. Useful convenience layer, not a thesis gap — the runbook's manual path works. Scope strictly to safe derived-store rebuilds; out of scope for the append-only ledger.

thesis_fit: Operator: migrate/restore/rebuild mutate all stores with no confirmation or --yes; migrate is forward-only with no rollback, so a mistaken migrate against prod has restore-from-backup as the only recovery. A fat-finger guard on irreversible ops is standard hygiene.
scope: src/cli.ts handleMigrations/handleRestore/handleRebuild. Prompt when interactive (TTY) unless --yes; always proceed under --yes/non-TTY for automation
dependencies: Cleanest once TTY detection exists (FEAT-CLI-003 shares the isTTY plumbing).
recommendation: Defer to v2.x (or fold into 2.0 if FEAT-CLI-003 lands). Keep non-blocking for automation — guard only the interactive path. Low priority but the irreversible-migrate/backup-only-recovery asymmetry makes it cheap insurance.

thesis_fit: Operator + app dev: large numerous immutable content (model outputs) has no durable home — the only durable backend is Postgres-canonical (no artifact support). S3 is the standard home for large content-addressed blobs (id IS the content hash = perfect S3 key), offloading bytes from DB + disk with real durability + scale.
scope: new src/adapters/s3/s3-artifact-store.ts (ArtifactStore get/getContent/put/list/delete); config.ts (artifact-backend selection + bucket/endpoint/creds); factory.ts (per-store backend wiring)
dependencies: Requires PER-STORE backend selection — config has a single backend enum today; an S3 artifact + Postgres canonical + SQLite index mix can't be expressed. That config refactor is the real prerequisite (itself breaking-ish). Pairs with streaming (FEAT-STORE-005). Adds an AWS SDK dep.
recommendation: Defer to v2.x. Build only once parity proves the per-store wiring AND a named user has large-artifact volume. The honest blocker is per-store backend config, not the S3 code. Do NOT build speculatively; the local adapter is adequate for dev/single-node today.

thesis_fit: AI agent: the index store is a case-insensitive substring scan with NO pluggability seam (IndexStore fixed; factory hardcodes LocalIndexStore), yet docs promise 'pluggable for richer backends (full-text, vector) later'. For semantic retrieval substring matching is the weakest possible search.
scope: src/contracts/index-store.ts (contract is already the seam — gap is implementations + config to select them); a SQLite FTS5 index store (folds into FEAT-STORE-006) for full-text; optionally a vector index store; docs/store-contracts.md:35-36
dependencies: Full-text comes nearly free with the SQLite adapter (FTS5) — strongly couple to FEAT-STORE-006. Vector adds an embedding dep and a much larger lift (touches retrieval semantics — out of scope for a storage pass).
recommendation: Partial implement-or-retract. v2.0: if SQLite (FEAT-STORE-006) ships, FTS5 makes the full-text-later half true; explicitly defer/soften the 'vector' word in store-contracts.md:36 until there's a real plan.
